briefdescription.para: clone clone the current node. The node is not associated with any image.
alphaLocked checks whether the node is a paint layer and returns whether it is alpha locked

simplesect.para: whether the paint layer is alpha locked, or false if the node is not a paint layer

briefdescription.para: setAlphaLocked set the layer to value if the node is paint layer.

simplesect.para: the blending mode of the layer. The values of the blending modes are defined in
KoCompositeOpRegistry.h

briefdescription.para: setBlendingMode set the blending mode of the node to the given value

parameterdescription.para: one of the string values from

simplesect.para: KoCompositeOpRegistry.h

briefdescription.para: channels creates a list of Channel objects that can be used individually to show or hide certain channels, and to retrieve the contents of each channel in a node separately.

detaileddescription.para: Only layers have channels, masks do not, and calling channels on a Node that is a mask will return an empty list.

simplesect.para: the list of channels ordered in by position of the channels in pixel position

briefdescription.para: childNodes

simplesect.para: returns a list of child nodes of the current node. The nodes are ordered from the bottommost up. The function is not recursive.

memberdef.argsstring: (const QString &name=QString(), bool recursive=false, bool partialMatch=false, const QString &type=QString(), int colorLabelIndex=0) const
memberdef.name: findChildNodes

briefdescription.para: findChildNodes

parameterdescription.para: name of the child node to search for. Leaving this blank will return all nodes.
whether or not to search recursively. Defaults to false.
return if the name partially contains the string (case insensitive). Defaults to false.
filter returned nodes based on type
filter returned nodes based on color label index

simplesect.para: returns a list of child nodes and grand child nodes of the current node that match the search criteria.

briefdescription.para: addChildNode adds the given node in the list of children.

parameterdescription.para: the node to be added
the node above which this node will be placed

simplesect.para: false if adding the node failed

briefdescription.para: removeChildNode removes the given node from the list of children.

parameterdescription.para: the node to be removed

briefdescription.para: setChildNodes this replaces the existing set of child nodes with the new set.

parameterdescription.para: The list of nodes that will become children, bottom-up the first node, is the bottom-most node in the stack.

detaileddescription.para:
colorDepth A string describing the color depth of the image:
U8: unsigned 8 bits integer, the most common type
U16: unsigned 16 bits integer
F16: half, 16 bits floating point. Only available if Krita was built with OpenEXR
F32: 32 bits floating point
the color depth.

briefdescription.para: colorModel retrieve the current color model of this document:

listitem.para: RGBA: RGB with alpha channel (The actual order of channels is most often BGR!)

simplesect.para: the internal color model string.
the name of the current color profile

briefdescription.para: setColorProfile set the color profile of the image to the given profile. The profile has to be registered with krita and be compatible with the current color model and depth; the image data is not converted.

simplesect.para: if assigning the color profile worked

briefdescription.para: setColorSpace convert the node to the given colorspace

parameterdescription.para:
A string describing the color model of the node:
A: Alpha mask
RGBA: RGB with alpha channel (The actual order of channels is most often BGR!)
XYZA: XYZ with alpha channel
LABA: LAB with alpha channel
CMYKA: CMYK with alpha channel
GRAYA: Gray with alpha channel
YCbCrA: YCbCr with alpha channel

A string describing the color depth of the image:
U8: unsigned 8 bits integer, the most common type
U16: unsigned 16 bits integer
F16: half, 16 bits floating point. Only available if Krita was built with OpenEXR
F32: 32 bits floating point
a valid color profile for this color model and color depth combination.

briefdescription.para: Krita layers can be animated, i.e., have frames.

simplesect.para: return true if the layer has frames. Currently, the scripting framework does not give access to the animation features.

briefdescription.para: enableAnimation make the current layer animated, so it can have frames.
Sets whether or not node should be pinned to the Timeline Docker, regardless of selection activity.

simplesect.para: Returns true if node is pinned to the Timeline Docker or false if it is not.

detaileddescription.para:
Sets the state of the node to the value of
collapsed
returns the collapsed state of this node
Sets a color label index associated to the layer. The actual color of the label and the number of available colors is defined by Krita GUI configuration.

briefdescription.para: setColorLabel sets a color label index associated to the layer. The actual color of the label and the number of available colors is defined by Krita GUI configuration.

parameterdescription.para: an integer corresponding to the set of available color labels.

briefdescription.para: inheritAlpha checks whether this node has the inherits alpha flag set

simplesect.para: true if the Inherit Alpha is set

detaileddescription.para: set the Inherit Alpha flag to the given value

briefdescription.para: locked checks whether the Node is locked. A locked node cannot be changed.

simplesect.para: true if the Node is locked, false if it hasn't been locked.

detaileddescription.para: set the Locked flag to the give value

briefdescription.para: does the node have any content in it?

simplesect.para: if node has any content in it
the user-visible name of this node.

detaileddescription.para: rename the Node to the given name
return the opacity of the Node. The opacity is a value between 0 and 255.
set the opacity of the Node to the given value. The opacity is a value between 0 and 255.
return the Node that is the parent of the current Node, or 0 if this is the root Node.

briefdescription.para: type Krita has several types of nodes, split in layers and masks. Group layers can contain other layers, any layer can contain masks.

detaileddescription.para:
The type of the node. Valid types are:
paintlayer
grouplayer
filelayer
filterlayer
filllayer
clonelayer
vectorlayer
transparencymask
filtermask
transformmask
selectionmask
colorizemask
If the Node object isn't wrapping a valid Krita layer or mask object, and empty string is returned.

briefdescription.para: icon

simplesect.para: the icon associated with the layer.

detaileddescription.para: Check whether the current Node is visible in the layer stack
Check to see if frame number on layer is a keyframe

Set the visibility of the current node to
visible

memberdef.argsstring: (int x, int y, int w, int h) const
memberdef.name: pixelData

briefdescription.para: pixelData reads the given rectangle from the Node's paintable pixels, if those exist, and returns it as a byte array. The pixel data starts top-left, and is ordered row-first.

detaileddescription.para: The byte array can be interpreted as follows: 8 bits images have one byte per channel, and as many bytes as there are channels. 16 bits integer images have two bytes per channel, representing an unsigned short. 16 bits float images have two bytes per channel, representing a half, or 16 bits float. 32 bits float images have four bytes per channel, representing a float.
You can read outside the node boundaries; those pixels will be transparent black.
The order of channels is:

listitem.para: Integer RGBA: Blue, Green, Red, Alpha
Float RGBA: Red, Green, Blue, Alpha
CMYKA: Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Key, Alpha

detaileddescription.para: The byte array is a copy of the original node data. In Python, you can use bytes, bytearray and the struct module to interpret the data and construct, for instance, a Pillow Image object.
If you read the pixeldata of a mask, a filter or generator layer, you get the selection bytes, which is one channel with values in the range from 0..255.

If you want to change the pixels of a node you can write the pixels back after manipulation with setPixelData(). This will only succeed on nodes with writable pixel data, e.g not on groups or file layers.

parameterdescription.para: x position from where to start reading
y position from where to start reading
row length to read
number of rows to read

simplesect.para: a QByteArray with the pixel data. The byte array may be empty.

memberdef.argsstring: (int x, int y, int w, int h, int time) const
memberdef.name: pixelDataAtTime

briefdescription.para: pixelDataAtTime a basic function to get pixeldata from an animated node at a given time.

parameterdescription.para: the position from the left to start reading.
the position from the top to start reader
the row length to read
the number of rows to read
the frame number

simplesect.para: a QByteArray with the pixel data. The byte array may be empty.

memberdef.argsstring: (int x, int y, int w, int h) const
memberdef.name: projectionPixelData

briefdescription.para: projectionPixelData reads the given rectangle from the Node's projection (that is, what the node looks like after all sub-Nodes (like layers in a group or masks on a layer) have been applied, and returns it as a byte array. The pixel data starts top-left, and is ordered row-first.

detaileddescription.para: The byte array can be interpreted as follows: 8 bits images have one byte per channel, and as many bytes as there are channels. 16 bits integer images have two bytes per channel, representing an unsigned short. 16 bits float images have two bytes per channel, representing a half, or 16 bits float. 32 bits float images have four bytes per channel, representing a float.
You can read outside the node boundaries; those pixels will be transparent black.
The order of channels is:

listitem.para: Integer RGBA: Blue, Green, Red, Alpha
Float RGBA: Red, Green, Blue, Alpha
CMYKA: Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, Key, Alpha

detaileddescription.para: The byte array is a copy of the original node data. In Python, you can use bytes, bytearray and the struct module to interpret the data and construct, for instance, a Pillow Image object.
If you read the projection of a mask, you get the selection bytes, which is one channel with values in the range from 0..255.

If you want to change the pixels of a node you can write the pixels back after manipulation with setPixelData(). This will only succeed on nodes with writable pixel data, e.g not on groups or file layers.

parameterdescription.para: x position from where to start reading
y position from where to start reading
row length to read
number of rows to read

simplesect.para: a QByteArray with the pixel data. The byte array may be empty.

memberdef.argsstring: (QByteArray value, int x, int y, int w, int h)
memberdef.name: setPixelData

briefdescription.para: setPixelData writes the given bytes, of which there must be enough, into the Node, if the Node has writable pixel data:

listitem.para: paint layer: the layer's original pixels are overwritten
filter layer, generator layer, any mask: the embedded selection's pixels are overwritten. Note: for these

detaileddescription.para: File layers, Group layers, Clone layers cannot be written to. Calling setPixelData on those layer types will silently do nothing.

parameterdescription.para: the byte array representing the pixels. There must be enough bytes available. Krita will take the raw pointer from the QByteArray and start reading, not stopping before (number of channels * size of channel * w * h) bytes are read.
the x position to start writing from
the y position to start writing from
the width of each row
the number of rows to write

simplesect.para: true if writing the pixeldata worked

briefdescription.para: bounds return the exact bounds of the node's paint device

simplesect.para: the bounds, or an empty QRect if the node has no paint device or is empty.

detaileddescription.para: move the pixels to the given x, y location in the image coordinate space.

briefdescription.para: position returns the position of the paint device of this node. The position is always 0,0 unless the layer has been moved. If you want to know the topleft position of the rectangle around the actual non-transparent pixels in the node, use bounds().

simplesect.para: the top-left position of the node

briefdescription.para: remove removes this node from its parent image.
duplicate returns a full copy of the current node. The node is not inserted in the graphic

simplesect.para: a valid Node object or 0 if the node couldn't be duplicated.

memberdef.argsstring: (const QString &filename, double xRes, double yRes, const InfoObject &exportConfiguration, const QRect &exportRect=QRect())
memberdef.name: save

briefdescription.para: save exports the given node with this filename. The extension of the filename determines the filetype.

parameterdescription.para: the filename including extension
the horizontal resolution in pixels per pt (there are 72 pts in an inch)
the horizontal resolution in pixels per pt (there are 72 pts in an inch)
a configuration object appropriate to the file format.

the export bounds for saving a node as a QRect If exportRect is empty, then save exactBounds() of the node. If you'd like to save the image- aligned area of the node, just pass image->bounds() there. See Document->exportImage for InfoObject details.

simplesect.para: true if saving succeeded, false if it failed.

briefdescription.para: mergeDown merges the given node with the first visible node underneath this node in the layerstack. This will drop all per-layer metadata.

memberdef.argsstring: (QPointF origin, int width, int height, QString strategy)
memberdef.name: scaleNode

briefdescription.para: scaleNode

parameterdescription.para: the origin point
the width
the height

the scaling strategy. There's several ones amongst these that aren't available in the regular UI.
Hermite
Bicubic - Adds pixels using the color of surrounding pixels. Produces smoother tonal gradations than Bilinear.
Box - Replicate pixels in the image. Preserves all the original detail, but can produce jagged effects.
Bilinear - Adds pixels averaging the color values of surrounding pixels. Produces medium quality results when the image is scaled from half to two times the original size.
Bell
BSpline
Lanczos3 - Offers similar results than Bicubic, but maybe a little bit sharper. Can produce light and dark halos along strong edges.
Mitchell

briefdescription.para: rotateNode rotate this layer by the given radians.

parameterdescription.para: amount the layer should be rotated in, in radians.

memberdef.argsstring: (int x, int y, int w, int h)
memberdef.name: cropNode

briefdescription.para: cropNode crop this layer.

parameterdescription.para: the left edge of the cropping rectangle.
the top edge of the cropping rectangle
the right edge of the cropping rectangle
the bottom edge of the cropping rectangle

briefdescription.para: shearNode perform a shear operation on this node.

parameterdescription.para: the X-angle in degrees to shear by
the Y-angle in degrees to shear by

briefdescription.para: thumbnail create a thumbnail of the given dimensions. The thumbnail is sized according to the layer dimensions, not the image dimensions. If the requested size is too big a null QImage is created. If the current node cannot generate a thumbnail, a transparent QImage of the requested size is generated.

simplesect.para: a QImage representing the layer contents.

briefdescription.para: layerStyleToAsl retrieve the current layer's style in ASL format.

simplesect.para: a QString in ASL format representing the layer style.

briefdescription.para: setLayerStyleFromAsl set a new layer style for this node.

parameterdescription.para: a string formatted in ASL format containing the layer style

simplesect.para: true if layer style was set, false if failed.

briefdescription.para: index the index of the node inside the parent

simplesect.para: an integer representing the node's index inside the parent

briefdescription.para: uniqueId uniqueId of the node

simplesect.para: a QUuid representing a unique id to identify the node

memberdef.argsstring: (const QPointF pointOne, const QPointF pointTwo, double pressureOne=1.0, double pressureTwo=1.0, const QString strokeStyle=PaintingResources::defaultStrokeStyle)
memberdef.name: paintLine

briefdescription.para: paint a line on the canvas. Uses current brush preset

parameterdescription.para: starting point
end point
starting pressure
end pressure

appearance of the outline, one of:
None - will use Foreground Color, since line would be invisible otherwise
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor

briefdescription.para: paint a rectangle on the canvas. Uses current brush preset

parameterdescription.para: QRect with x, y, width, and height

appearance of the outline, one of:
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Default is ForegroundColor.

appearance of the fill, one of:
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Pattern
Default is None.

memberdef.argsstring: (const QList< QPointF > points, const QString strokeStyle=PaintingResources::defaultStrokeStyle, const QString fillStyle=PaintingResources::defaultFillStyle)
memberdef.name: paintPolygon

briefdescription.para: paint a polygon on the canvas. Uses current brush preset

parameterdescription.para:
of Qpoints
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Default is ForegroundColor.

appearance of the fill, one of:
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Pattern
Default is None.

briefdescription.para: paint an ellipse on the canvas. Uses current brush preset

parameterdescription.para:
QRect with x, y, width, and height
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Default is ForegroundColor.

appearance of the fill, one of:
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Pattern
Default is None.

briefdescription.para: paint a custom path on the canvas. Uses current brush preset

parameterdescription.para:
QPainterPath to determine path
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Default is ForegroundColor.

appearance of the fill, one of:
None
ForegroundColor
BackgroundColor
Pattern
Default is None.

briefdescription.para: paintAbility can be used to determine whether this node can be painted on with the current brush preset.

simplesect.para:
QString, one of the following:
VECTOR - This node is vector-based.
CLONE - This node is a Clone Layer.
PAINT - This node is paintable by the current brush preset.
UNPAINTABLE - This node is not paintable, or a null preset is somehow selected./li>
MYPAINTBRUSH_UNPAINTABLE - This node's non-RGBA colorspace cannot be painted on by the currently selected MyPaint brush.

briefdescription.para: paintDevice gives access to the internal paint device of this Node

simplesect.para: the paintdevice or 0 if the node does not have an editable paint device.

detaileddescription.para: Node represents a layer or mask in a Krita image's Node hierarchy. Group layers can contain other layers and masks; layers can contain masks.
